titlestmt.titleproper: Medical Society of the State of New York records2695
titlestmt.author: Compiled by Jane Gustafson

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2020-09-23 18:11:46 -0400.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Medical Society of the State of New York records

origination.label: creator
origination.corpname: Medical Society of the State of New York

did.physdesc: Minute meetings, correspondence, records, newsletters, journals, photographs.
did.abstract: Includes minutes of meetings and agenda of several councils, in addition to membership records and publications.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: ORGANIZATIONAL HISTORY
bioghist.p: The Medical Society of the State of New York was created by Chapter 138 of the Laws of 1806.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Includes minutes of meetings and agenda of the Executive Council, 1934-1973; records of annual meetings of the House of Delegates, 1931-1971; minutes of meetings of Trustees, 1941-1965; President's files, mostly correspondence of Norman S. Moore, 1960-1961; membership records, 1945-1959; publications, including New York Journal of Medicine, 1910-1972, Transactions of the Medical Society of the State of New York, 1807-1903, The Reflex, 1966-1967, newsletters, journals, and other printed records; subject files; committee files; records of conventions; tape recordings; biographical sketches; photographs; records of the medical societies of Erie County (1801-1951) and Kings County (1822-1972); a "Safeguard Your Health" series of 8 reels of movie film and three phonograph records; and newspaper clippings collected by the society's Public Relations Department.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Medical Society of the State of New York records, #2695. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

accessrestrict.head: Access Restrictions:
accessrestrict.p: Access restricted to the permission of the President or the Chairman of the Board of Trustees, or the Executive Vice-President, of the Medical Society of the State of New York.

controlaccess.subject: Medicine -- Societies, etc. -- New York (State) -- Kings County (N.Y.)
Medicine -- Societies, etc. -- New York (State) -- Erie County.
Medicine -- Societies, etc.
controlaccess.title: Safeguard your health.
The reflex.
Transactions of the medical society of the state of New York.
New York journal of medicine.

unittitle.extref: Link to a partial finding aid. PDF file.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Not all the material in the collection is covered by this guide.

did.unittitle: Series I. Public Health and Education
c. Revised report BCG advisory committee
a. Cancer subcommittee meeting between the State Health Department and the Ulster County Medical Society
b. Report by Dr. Adie of the Rip Van Winkle Clinic in Columbia County
c. Program plan for the control of cancer from the New York State Department of Health
c. Program plan for the control of cancer from the New York City Department of Health
d. Breast self examination pamphlet "Cancers and Detection in the Doctor's Office"
o. Subcommittee correspondence concerning cancer control

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains November 13, 1947 report of the subcommitte, material concerning the American Cancer Society and correspondence between Dr. Theodore Curphey and Dr. Victor C. Jacobsen concerning the establishment of cancer detection clinics in Rensselaer, Ulster and Nassau counties.

did.unittitle: q. Correspondence and reports from Dr. Odie, chairman of the subcommittee on cancer
r. Meetings in New York City
s. Revised treatment correspondence and printed items
Neonatal lectures at the New York Academy of Medicine
Retrolental Fibroplasia (premature babies) correspondence and mimeographs
Child welfare subcommittee correspondence and mimeographs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence,typescript, mimeographed and printed materials concerning practice of chiropractic basic science law.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This folder is empty.

did.unittitle: c. Diabetes self-testing resolution and correspondence

scopecontent.p: Conference in New York City

c. Correspondence with Dr. Frederic D. Zunaro, chairman

scopecontent.p: Includes 1950 progress report and 1948 article.

c. Estimated budget for center, correspondence

a. Beekman Downtown Hospital correspondence concerning the use of psychologists and social workers
c. Report on state mental hospitals system
d. Mental hygiene subcommittee and the cerebral palsy subcommittee, correspondence

d. "Normal and Modified Diets: a Manual for Professional Personnel"
a. Physical medicine and rehabilitation subcommittee, correspondence with members
b. Physical medicine and rehabilitation subcommittee, correspondence and meeting minutes
e. Questionnaire, survey subcommittee concerning rehabilitation facilities
g. Lists of members, fee schedules for physicians and surgeons, correspondence concerning federal-state programs
a. Correspondence by chairman and secretaries largely concerned with Rheumatic Fever Program Plan of New York State Department of Health
c. Minutes of the executive committee, American Council on Rheumatic Fever of the American Hearth Association
c. Correspondence of Dr. Theodore J. Curphey concerning the 1952 Heart Bulletin and county programs
d. Rheumatic fever program correspondence and mimeographed and printed material, talks and lectures concerning diagnosis and treatment
d. Minutes of conference at Metropolitan Life Insurance Company in New York City
a. Correspondence of Dr. I.G. Fred Hirs, chairman of the subcommittee on 4-H clubs and youth health activities
a. Outline for use in obstetrics and gynecology by Dr. R. Gordon Douglas

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

did.unittitle: b. Therapy speakers outline on sulfonamide therapy, a course of three, two hour sessions

g. Correspondence and other material concerning school health

l. Opinions expressed and submitted to committee
m. Reallocation of school funds; regents inquiry and correspondence concerning both subjects
a. Material concerning the subcommittee on blood and plasma exchange bank

did.unittitle: Series VI. Directories, Notebooks, Scrapbooks, Printed Items

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notebook compiled by Doris K. Dougherty lists members, committees, delegations, publications, etc. of the Medical Society of the State of New York.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notebook compiled by Doris K. Dougherty lists the various officers, delegates, committee members, councilmen and executive positions.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notebook compiled by Doris K. Dougherty lists all of the activities and participants of the Medical Society of the State of New York and includes a program of the annual meeting, a bulletin of the annual banquet, and a carbon copy of the minutes for the 149th annual meeting.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notebook compiled by Doris K.Dougherty lists all of the activities and participants of the Medical Society of the State of New York.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lists members of the various sections of the Medical Society of the State of New York and the activities of the group.

scopecontent.p: Corresopndence concerns change in dues policy and includes American Medical Association lists, Education Fund and War Memorial Fund files.

did.unittitle: Series VIII. New York State Journal of Medicine Editorial Files

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes manuscript record forms for each article, correspondence between authors and George W. Komak , MD, managing editor, reprint requests, illustration arrangements and some copies of articles.

did.unittitle: 11 inch x 14 inch sheets show the makeup of each issue

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Major headings are accident prevention and aging and nursing homes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes files on alcoholism, drug abuse, American Medical Association archives, annual reports, ambulance services, Albion A. Bernstein Awards, budget, cancer.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: includes files on cardiovascular and chronic pulmonary diseases, continuing education committee and program and comprehensive health program.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence, reprints and booklets relating to county medical societies, continuing education, resuscitation course book, and continuing public information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes files on data processing, empire state and emergency rooms.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes files on environmental medicine, epilispy, forensic medicine hypnotism and hopitals.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes files on infectious diseases, legislation (perinatal study, adoptions), maternal and child welfare,and mental hygiene.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Files include mental hygiene (continued), Dr. Norman S. Moore, and multiple screening.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes files on New York State Health Council, New York State Health Department, and Public Health and Education

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Files include public health and education, quackery, school health, and smoking.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Files include speeches, state medical society mailings, and rural medical services and folders out of order: heart disease, state cancer committee, division of vocational rehabilitation, community health services.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes voting for amendment in 1962

Series X. Photographs, Tape Recordings, Films and Artifacts
Photos of John Stearns, John Steel, Tabor B. Reynolds, George Foster Comstock
Photos of Dr. Percy G. Waller, Dr. Oliver Austin, Dr. Mary J. Ross

scopecontent.p: Eight reels of film and three records.
